content.question: How much of Hungary's current performance plateau (PISA scores near OECD average) is attributable to tracking structure versus teacher supply/quality, curriculum coherence, and pandemic recovery dynamics, and would dismantling tracks alone improve outcomes without other system reforms?
content.reduction_path: Comparative decomposition analysis: estimate structural equation models predicting PISA outcomes including tracking prevalence, teacher characteristics (qualification, experience), curriculum measure, and school resource variables in Hungary and peer early-tracking countries; conduct synthetic counterfactual removing early tracking while holding other features constant; compare to actual outcomes in countries that abolished early tracking while preserving other institutional features.
